hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by optimizing position 5 for aromatic complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 residues (192/193/195/197) and position 11 for steric differentiation from SSTR3/5 subtypes will increase the selectivity margin while preserving strong SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but fail to sufficiently disrupt off-target interactions. The next iteration should focus on targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on these positions allows for targeted mutation to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore.
Proposing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) pharmacophore and Cys3/Cys14 disulfide.
